channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Reaction from Rio after Brazil's last-gasp World Cup win against Japan
item.description: Gabriel ⁠Martinelli ⁠scored the winning goal in the ⁠fifth minute of ​second-half stoppage time and Brazil climbed out of a halftime hole to send Japan packing 2-1 in a World Cup ​round of 32 match Monday in Houston. France 24's Jan Onoszko has the reaction from Rio.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Shoppers to pay the price after heatwave hits French crops
item.description: The heatwave that has swept across Europe in recent days has left farmers in France warning that many of their crops are likely to take a severe hit from the extreme weather - meaning prices are likely to rise for many supermarket goods.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Trump says Iran meeting set in Qatar despite Tehran's denials
item.description: US President Donald Trump said Iran requested a meeting on Tuesday in Qatar, despite Tehran denying any direct negotiations were planned with Washington on the deal aimed at ending the Middle East war. Washington and Tehran both have said they were sending teams to the Gulf state, but issued conflicting statements on most other details, including timing and purpose of the trip.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Venezuela rescuers in final push to find survivors as anger mounts
item.description: Rescuers from around the world pushed Tuesday to save any final survivors trapped under mountains of rubble after Venezuela's massive quakes that killed over 1,700 people, with tens of thousands still unaccounted for. Meanwhile, anger is growing over the government's slow and limited aid in a country mired in a deep crisis that has driven millions to emigrate in recent years.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Monaco blast wounds several with Ukrainian oligarch the believed target
item.description: Police in Monaco and neighbouring France were searching on Tuesday for a man suspected of detonating a makeshift bomb in ​Monaco ‌that wounded several people, a local official said, ⁠while French and Ukrainian media reported that a Ukrainian-born oligarch was the intended target.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Spain: More than one million undocumented migrants apply for amnesty scheme
item.description: Over one million undocumented migrants in Spain have applied for legal status under a vast regularisation scheme launched in April that grants those eligible the right to live and work in the country. The window to apply for the programme closes Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Ukraine's war-damaged power grid struggles under soaring heat
item.description: Ukraine's energy grid is struggling to cope amid soaring temperatures as the war-torn country is hit by the deadly heatwave that has afflicted much of Europe over the last week. Russian drone and missile attacks have decimated Ukraine's energy network since Moscow invaded in February 2022 -- causing tens of billions of dollars worth of damage and leading to frequent power outages in the coldest and hottest parts of the year, as it cannot handle surges in demand.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Supreme Court to rule on Trump's bid to roll back birthright citizenship
item.description: Decisions are expected this week from the US supreme court that will determine just how far Donald Trump can flex his executive power. But one of the most awaited rulings is on the president's efforts to roll back birthright citizenship. The case was argued in April and a final ruling is awaited on Tuesday. Caroline Baum explains.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Meet your MEP! Behind the scenes of the European Parliament
item.description: You might know that the European Parliament is the only directly elected institution in the EU, and that it plays a crucial role as a co-legislator with the EU Council. But what does an MEP actually do? We meet three Belgian MEPs from contrasting political ideologies and parties. Through them, we explore some of the key European issues that they are working on.
item.dc:creator: Armen GEORGIAN

item.title: French heatwave threatens food inflation as agriculture sector suffers
item.description: Tomatoes cook on the vine and cereal crops get scorched – climate change is hitting French agriculture, and shoppers are poised to bear the brunt of it. Also, the EU and China set an October deadline to make progress on trade disputes, while the ECB Chief Christine Lagarde says the Eurozone economy is resilient enough to withstand an end to exceptional monetary policy measures.
item.dc:creator: Bryan QUINN

item.title: Shock gives way to anger in Venezuela as Rodríguez faces quake response criticism
item.description: Frustration is rising across Venezuela over a lack of government help in areas struck by deadly twin earthquakes last week, with miracle rescues increasingly rare and the death toll hitting more than 1,700 on Monday. France 24's Philip Turle takes a look at how president Delcy Rodriguez has dealt with the crisis.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Paraguay stun Germany in round of 32
item.description: Paraguay made history by beating Germany in the World Cup round of 16 (1–1, 4-3 on penalties). Paraguay could face France in the next round.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: South Africa deploys police, foreigners brace for violence as anti-immigrant protests begin
item.description: Shops shuttered, workers stayed home and police fanned out across South Africa on Tuesday as fears mounted that planned anti-immigrant protests would descend into violence. Thousands of foreigners already fled ahead of the unauthorised June 30 "deadline" set by anti-immigrant groups for all migrants to leave the country.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Final dash for documents as Spain triggers largest migrant amnesty in European history
item.description: A vast migrant regularisation launched by Spain's leftist government in defiance of a growing European crackdown on irregular immigration ends on Tuesday, with the final hopefuls scrambling to secure their legal status. The government has said more than one million people have applied. France 24's correspondent in Madrid Sarah Morris has the latest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Discover the winners of FRANCE 24's video competition on EU cohesion policy
item.description: It's one of the EU's most important instruments, but one of the least talked-about ones: cohesion policy aims to reduce disparities between Europe's regions, and make sure no part of Europe is left behind. We commissioned a video competition, inviting young people from high schools, universities and journalism schools to take part.
item.dc:creator: Caroline DE CAMARET

item.title: Discover the winners of FRANCE 24's video competition on fighting fake news
item.description: It's no secret that the EU is caught in a maelstrom of disinformation aimed at undermining its credibility. In response, FRANCE 24 commissioned a video competition, inviting high school pupils, university students and journalism school students to take part.
item.dc:creator: Caroline DE CAMARET

item.title: On the ground in Venezuela: 'All the buildings have collapsed'
item.description: France 24's Maxime Pluvinet reports from La Guaira state, one of the areas hardest hit by the twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela on June 24. "All the buildings have collapsed" he says, whle "Venezuelans are still waiting for news from their loved ones trapped under the rubble".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: France vow to maintain attacking philosophy against Sweden
item.description: France's fearsome attack carried them smoothly through the World Cup group stage, but coach Didier Deschamps warned his players to remain "humble" as they play their first knockout match on Tuesday against a Swedish side that scored five goals in their tournament opener and then conceded as many in their following game.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: How ships are risking ‘safe’ passage through the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A plan to divert shipping through an alternative corridor off the Omani coast to avoid the threat of mines in the Strait of Hormuz has come under scrutiny after two attacks on ships in recent days. Though Iran and Oman agreed to engage in talks on Monday on the strait's management, the incidents illustrate the complexity of maintaining navigation through the crucial waterway, which remains at the forefront of the standoff between Tehran and Washington.
item.dc:creator: Bahar MAKOOI

item.title: World Cup 2026: Morocco beat Netherlands on penalties to reach round of 16
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Morocco beat Netherlands on penalties to reach round of 16
item.description: Morocco booked their place in the World Cup round of 16 after defeating the Netherlands on penalties following a dramatic 1-1 draw after extra time in Monterrey. Cody Gakpo gave the Dutch the lead before Issa Diop headed home a stoppage-time equaliser, with Ismael Saibari converting the decisive spot-kick to send the Atlas Lions through.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Middle East live: Qatar says no direct US-Iran talks to take place in Doha
item.description: Mediator Qatar said on Tuesday that no high-level meetings or direct talks between the United States and Iran were planned in Doha after Washington said it had sent a top delegation for negotiations. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Venezuela's La Guaira port reopens to deliver earthquake aid, US military says
item.description: Venezuela's La Guaira port has resumed operations after repairs and is receiving humanitarian aid for earthquake victims, the US military said Monday. US Southern Command said the USS Fort Lauderdale was delivering critical supplies through the port after twin powerful earthquakes killed more than 1,700 people and left tens of thousands missing.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24
